raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/year> I took a private taxi from hotel in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX to the airport. The agreed price was {$46.00} USD. At the end of the ride, the driver had me scan my credit card using a reader attached to his phone. He showed me the agreed price of {$46.00} USD in the XXXX peso equivalent before charging my card. However the initial card I used was being declined due to this being an unrecognized foreign transaction. This happened twice, so I switched cards. The first 2 times the driver showed me the price, but the last time he did not. I am not sure if it was intentional or a mistake by the driver as he was rushing, but he charged my card {$460.00} USD for the taxi ride, adding an extra digit to the end of the charge. This time using the second card, the charge was accepted the first time and the payment processed. I did not realize until in the airport when connected to WiFi of the charged amount. When I disputed the claim with my credit card company, and provided the screenshot proof of the original declined transaction they said they can not issue me a credit due to it being not enough proof. I was told to contact the merchant, however it was a taxi, not uber and I had no way of identifying the driver or company. My logic is, I am providing the declined charge message from the initial attempt showing the agreed price that was attempted to be charged. However my card was not charged that amount. All I want to do is pay the agreed amount that I was originally charged had it not been declined. Since this was in a foreign country, with a language barrier I was unable to get more documentation. I reopened the dispute with the credit card company, and wrote them a letter with a printout of the screenshot showing the dispute charge and I received another denial letter from them one month later. The credit card company in question is JetBlue Mastercard, which is unfortunate due to my loyalty to JetBlue for my travels.
